commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Clothing us from His own store

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: I counsel you to buy of me gold tried in the fire, that you may be rich; and white clothing, that you may be clothed, and that the shame of your nakedness does not appear; and anoint your eyes with eye salve, that you may see. Victorinus of Pettau That what you gladly know by the Scripture, you should strive also to do the work of the same. And because, if in these ways men return out of great destruction to great repentance, they are not only useful to themselves, but they are able also to be of advantage to many, He promised them no small reward—to sit, namely, on the throne of judgment.

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: That is, that in whatever manner you can, you should suffer for the Lord's name tribulations and passions.
